keyPoints: Frame structured reflective prompts as the core mechanism shaping depth of processing and durable retention
Prioritize a six-week randomized controlled design with two parallel arms and baseline stratification by SRL/metacognition
Specify primary outcomes: learning depth and retention, plus secondary outcomes: relationship satisfaction and SRL processes
Plan robust measurement: validated instruments, transfer tasks, delayed post-tests, and cognitive load metrics
Integrate social-dimension considerations carefully, using optional social presence cues while monitoring cognitive load and bias
Anticipate failure modes (over-trust, homogenization, mode collapse) and articulate concrete countermeasures (deliberate difficulty, diverse outputs, reflective prompts)
